articles.title: Hasbro-Branded Family Entertainment Center Coming to Pigeon Forge in 2027
articles.summary: A Hasbro-themed family entertainment center is set to open in Pigeon Forge, Tennessee in summer 2027, bringing major IP-driven FEC development to one of the country's top family tourism markets. The announcement signals continued growth in branded FEC concepts leveraging well-known toy and game intellectual property. FEC operators should watch this development as IP-licensing partnerships become an increasingly competitive differentiator in the sector.

articles.title: WildBrain CPLG to Expand Miraculous IP into Location-Based Entertainment Across EMEA and APAC
articles.summary: WildBrain CPLG has been appointed to develop location-based entertainment opportunities for the popular Miraculous brand across Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and Asia-Pacific. This deal opens doors for FEC operators in those regions to pursue licensing agreements for a franchise with a massive youth following. The move reflects a broader trend of entertainment IP holders actively seeking FEC and LBE partners to extend brand reach.

articles.title: Universal Kids Resort in Frisco, Texas Previews Attractions Ahead of July 1 Opening
articles.summary: Universal's first regional theme park designed exclusively for young children opens July 1 in Frisco, Texas, featuring seven themed lands tied to DreamWorks, Nickelodeon, and Illumination IP across 20 acres. The opening of a major branded family destination in the Dallas-Fort Worth suburb will intensify competition for family entertainment spend in one of the country's fastest-growing metro areas. FEC operators in the region should anticipate a shift in family outing patterns and consider how to differentiate their offerings.

articles.title: TrainerTainment to Present Bowling Center Growth Strategies at Bowl Expo 2026
articles.summary: TrainerTainment, powered by The GrowthPro Group, will exhibit at Bowl Expo 2026 in Nashville (June 28–July 2) with a focus on delivering real-world bowling center growth case studies. The session is directly relevant to bowling FEC operators looking for proven revenue and operational improvement strategies. Bowl Expo remains the premier gathering for bowling industry professionals and a key networking event for FEC suppliers.

articles.title: Slick City Action Park Opens at Potomac Mills Mall in Northern Virginia
articles.summary: Slick City Action Park, an indoor slide and active entertainment concept, has opened a new location at Potomac Mills, one of the Washington D.C. area's largest shopping malls. The opening is part of Slick City's aggressive retail-anchored expansion strategy and adds to the growing number of active FEC concepts filling anchor and junior-anchor mall spaces. FEC operators and developers should take note of this model as indoor activity parks continue to thrive in mall environments.

articles.title: New Adventure Park Billed as Unlike Any Other in the Country Opens in Atlanta
articles.summary: A new adventure park has opened in Atlanta, positioning itself as a unique offering in the crowded Southeast indoor entertainment market. Atlanta continues to attract significant FEC investment given its large family demographic and strong tourism base. Operators considering expansion in the Southeast should monitor how this venue differentiates itself and performs.

articles.title: Plymouth, UK to Get Large New Go-Kart and Laser Tag Entertainment Hub
articles.summary: A major new entertainment venue featuring go-karts, laser tag, and other FEC attractions is planned for Plymouth, England, adding to the wave of multi-activity venues opening across the UK. The development reflects continued investor appetite for large-format FEC concepts in secondary UK cities. Suppliers of go-kart and laser tag equipment should view this as a pipeline opportunity.

articles.title: Multi-Million-Pound Entertainment Venue Opens at Brent Cross Shopping Centre in London
articles.summary: A new large-scale entertainment venue has opened at Brent Cross shopping centre in London, continuing the trend of premium leisure operators anchoring major retail destinations. The UK FEC market is seeing sustained investment in mixed-use leisure-retail formats as mall owners seek entertainment to drive footfall. This opening is relevant for both UK operators and international investors tracking the health of the European FEC market.

articles.title: New Family Entertainment Facility Set to Open in Springfield, Illinois in July
articles.summary: A new entertainment facility is scheduled to open in Springfield, Illinois this July, adding another FEC option to a mid-sized Midwest market. New openings in secondary markets highlight the continued geographic spread of FEC investment beyond major metros. Local operators should assess how the new venue affects competitive dynamics in the region.

articles.title: High Tide VR Near Bethany Beach Offers Immersive Virtual Reality Entertainment
articles.summary: High Tide VR, a virtual reality entertainment venue near Bethany Beach, Delaware, is offering a distinctive LBE experience in a beach vacation market typically dominated by outdoor attractions. The venue demonstrates the viability of VR-based FEC concepts in seasonal resort markets where weather can drive guests indoors. FEC operators in tourist areas may find this a useful case study for diversifying revenue with technology-driven attractions.

articles.title: Lululand Adventure Park to Make Pennsylvania Debut in 2026
articles.summary: Lululand Adventure Park is expanding into Pennsylvania, bringing its indoor adventure concept to a new state market. The brand's geographic expansion reflects broader growth in the indoor adventure park sector as operators scale proven concepts into new regions. FEC investors and developers tracking emerging multi-location brands should add Lululand to their watchlist.

articles.title: Masti Zone Opens New Family Entertainment Destination in Greater Noida, India
articles.summary: Masti Zone has launched a new family entertainment center at PKS Town Central in Greater Noida West, targeting the rapidly growing urban family market in India's NCR region. The opening underscores accelerating FEC development across India's tier-two and satellite cities as disposable incomes and mall development expand. International FEC suppliers and franchisors should take note of India as an increasingly significant growth market.

articles.title: Interstellar Arc at Area15 Wins 'Best Location-Based Entertainment' at Auggie Awards
articles.summary: Interstellar Arc, the immersive XR experience at Area15 in Las Vegas created by Felix & Paul Studios, has won Best Location-Based Entertainment at the 17th annual Auggie Awards, which recognize excellence in augmented and extended reality. The recognition highlights the growing intersection of premium XR technology and location-based entertainment as a differentiator for high-end FEC and LBE venues. FEC operators exploring immersive technology attractions should study Interstellar Arc as a benchmark for award-winning guest experience design.

articles.title: Area15 Opens Eye Dropper, a 200-Foot Psychedelic Drop Tower Experience in Las Vegas
articles.summary: Area15 has opened Eye Dropper, a 200-foot-tall drop tower reimagined as a cinematic, story-driven thrill experience set within its expanding Zone 2 entertainment district in Las Vegas. The attraction is part of a broader expansion strategy that has driven double-digit visitation growth at the venue. The launch illustrates how experiential storytelling layered onto traditional thrill rides can elevate guest experience and support premium pricing.

articles.title: Thrillz Atlanta Indoor Theme Park: Operational Insights for FEC Operators
articles.summary: A detailed look at Thrillz Atlanta, an indoor theme park concept, examines what FEC operators can learn from its model, attractions mix, and operational approach. The piece is positioned as a practical resource for operators considering similar large-format indoor entertainment investments. FEC professionals evaluating indoor theme park expansion should review this analysis for benchmarking.

articles.title: Largest Indoor Slide Attraction Positions Itself as Alternative to Disney World for Family Tourism
articles.summary: A major indoor slide attraction is being promoted as a compelling alternative to destination theme parks for family travelers, challenging traditional assumptions about what competes with Disney World in the family tourism market. The development is relevant for FEC operators as it demonstrates how indoor attractions can attract destination travelers, not just local repeat guests. This shift in consumer perception could open new marketing and positioning opportunities for large-format FEC venues.

articles.title: Fort Worth's Tornado Terry's Family Amusement Center Closes After Three Decades
articles.summary: Tornado Terry's Family Amusement Center in Fort Worth, Texas, a community fixture for nearly 30 years, is closing its doors, citing challenges common to legacy independent FEC operators. The closure is a reminder of the ongoing pressure facing older, single-location FEC businesses competing against newer, larger entertainment concepts. Industry stakeholders should take note of the lessons this closure offers around facility reinvestment and competitive positioning.

articles.title: Family-Owned Fort Worth FEC to Shut Down After 30 Years
articles.summary: A longtime family-owned entertainment business in Fort Worth, Texas is closing after three decades of operation, reflecting broader challenges for independent FEC operators without the capital to modernize. The story highlights the sustainability pressures facing legacy amusement centers as consumer expectations for facility quality and attraction variety have risen sharply. FEC operators in similar situations should consider reinvestment strategies or partnership opportunities before reaching a closure decision.

articles.title: Popular Entertainment Chain Eyeing New Location in Missoula, Montana
articles.summary: A popular entertainment chain is reportedly considering a new location in Missoula, Montana, signaling possible FEC expansion into smaller Mountain West markets. While details remain limited, the report is relevant for operators and suppliers tracking which brands are actively growing their footprints into underserved mid-size cities. Secondary market expansion continues to be a key theme in the FEC sector as major metro markets become increasingly saturated.

articles.title: Arizona Ranks Top 10 Indoor Entertainment Attractions for 2026
articles.summary: AZ Big Media has published its ranking of Arizona's top 10 indoor entertainment attractions for 2026, offering a snapshot of the competitive indoor FEC and entertainment landscape in the Phoenix metro and surrounding areas. Rankings like these influence consumer decision-making and are a useful benchmarking tool for operators assessing their market position. FEC venues in Arizona should evaluate their standing and consider what improvements might earn future recognition.

articles.title: Land of Make Believe Owner Chris Maier Recognized for Decades of FEC Leadership
articles.summary: Chris Maier, who transformed Land of Make Believe in New Jersey into a successful family entertainment destination over more than five decades, has been recognized for his outstanding contributions to the FEC industry. His story offers a case study in long-term vision, operational discipline, and community-focused programming for independent FEC operators. The recognition serves as an inspiration for owner-operators navigating the challenges of sustaining multi-generational family entertainment businesses.

articles.title: Ten Key Principles for Translating Intellectual Property into Immersive FEC Experiences
articles.summary: A detailed guide outlines 10 critical principles for successfully designing location-based and immersive entertainment experiences built around licensed IP, covering stakeholder alignment, brand integrity, fan expectations, and operational approval processes. As IP-driven FEC concepts grow in popularity, operators considering branded partnerships will find this a practical framework for navigating complex licensor relationships. The article is essential reading for FEC developers and creative directors working on or evaluating IP-based attraction projects.

articles.title: Expert Guide to Maximizing Footfall and Revenue with Travelling Exhibitions
articles.summary: A comprehensive guide for venue operators covers how to select, programme, and monetize travelling exhibitions, with practical advice on commercial readiness, visitor flow, tactile interaction design, and contract structuring. While aimed broadly at cultural venues, the lessons on turnkey marketing packages, revenue sharing, and modular layout design are directly applicable to FEC operators considering pop-up or touring attractions. This is a useful operational resource for FEC venues looking to supplement permanent attractions with rotating experiences.

articles.title: Black Diamond FEC's Full Throttle Saturday Delivers Strong Father's Day Event
articles.summary: Black Diamond's Full Throttle Saturday event was highlighted as a standout Father's Day experience, offering a model for how FEC operators can build themed event programming around calendar holidays to drive incremental revenue. Holiday-specific events remain one of the most effective tools for FEC operators to boost visits during typically slower mid-year periods. Operators looking to grow group and event revenue should study Black Diamond's programming approach.

articles.title: Fun Spot America Plans Major Fireworks Spectacle for America's 250th Independence Day
articles.summary: Fun Spot America is marking the United States' 250th anniversary with large-scale fireworks displays at both its Orlando and Kissimmee parks, capitalizing on the historic national celebration to drive guest visits. The event demonstrates how FEC operators can align programming with major cultural milestones to create compelling marketing hooks and boost attendance. Operators planning summer events should consider how America250 celebrations can be leveraged for similar promotional impact.
